message:
test(conformance): drop CONFIG SET numeric XFAIL entries that are now aligned with upstream; refresh closed beads

crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs
  - Remove seven entries from the CONFIG SET XFAIL skiplist that
    previously documented our divergent error wording against
    upstream redis-server:
        config_set_maxmemory_negative_error
        config_set_maxmemory_non_integer_error
        config_set_maxmemory_float_error
        config_set_acllog_negative_error
        config_set_acllog_non_integer_error
        config_set_slowlog_max_len_negative_error
        config_set_maxmemory_policy_invalid
    All seven now round-trip against the live upstream oracle with
    byte-for-byte identical reply text (fr-runtime's CONFIG SET
    numeric validator has been aligned in prior commits). Leaving
    them on the XFAIL list would mask a future regression that
    re-introduces the old wording.
  - The surrounding comment block that described the divergence
    ("upstream emits the long-form 'CONFIG SET failed (possibly
    related to argument …)'…") is removed alongside the entries it
    documented. The skiplist still retains the genuinely
    unaligned entries (e.g. `active-defrag-enabled` which we do
    not model).

.beads/issues.jsonl
  - Routine beads flush: updates the closed_at/updated_at and
    close_reason on frankenredis-04mo (BLMPOP numkeys wording
    finally fixed; rest already resolved), frankenredis-19ic
    (ZINTERCARD/ZMPOP/BZMPOP + ZRANGEBYSCORE LIMIT already
    resolved — live_redis_core_zset passes 100% under STRICT),
    and a couple of adjacent close-outs. No structural schema
    change; this is just the latest `br sync --flush-only`
    output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -9066,20 +9066,6 @@ mod tests {
             "config_rewrite",
             "config_rewrite_ok",
             "config_subcommand_case_insensitive_rewrite",
-            // CONFIG SET numeric-range errors: upstream emits the
-            // long-form "CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to
-            // argument '...') - argument must be a memory value /
-            // between 0 and ... inclusive / couldn't be parsed" while
-            // ours emits the generic "value is not an integer" or the
-            // per-parameter custom message. Fix in fr-runtime
-            // CONFIG SET numeric validator.
-            "config_set_maxmemory_negative_error",
-            "config_set_maxmemory_non_integer_error",
-            "config_set_maxmemory_float_error",
-            "config_set_acllog_negative_error",
-            "config_set_acllog_non_integer_error",
-            "config_set_slowlog_max_len_negative_error",
-            "config_set_maxmemory_policy_invalid",
             // active-defrag-enabled is a feature we don't model;
             // upstream accepts the parameter. Silent-accept vs
             // reject semantic mismatch.
